lines.english: Lesson 25
lines.chinese: 第25课
lines.raw: Lesson 25 | 第25课

lines.english: The Cutty Sark
lines.chinese: 卡蒂萨克号
lines.raw: The Cutty Sark | 卡蒂萨克号

lines.english: What piece of bad luck prevented the Cutty Sark from winning the race?
lines.chinese: 什么厄运阻止了卡蒂萨克号赢得比赛？
lines.raw: What piece of bad luck prevented the Cutty Sark from winning the race? | 什么厄运阻止了卡蒂萨克号赢得比赛？

lines.english: One of the most famous sailing ships of the nineteenth century, the Cutty Sark, can still be seen at Greenwich.
lines.chinese: 19世纪最著名的帆船之一，“卡蒂萨克”号至今仍可在格林威治见到。
lines.raw: One of the most famous sailing ships of the nineteenth century, the Cutty Sark, can still be seen at Greenwich. | 19世纪最著名的帆船之一，“卡蒂萨克”号至今仍可在格林威治见到。

lines.english: She stands on dry land and is visited by thousands of people each year.
lines.chinese: 她停泊在陆地上，每年都有成千上万的人参观。
lines.raw: She stands on dry land and is visited by thousands of people each year. | 她停泊在陆地上，每年都有成千上万的人参观。

lines.english: She serves as an impressive reminder of the great ships of the past.
lines.chinese: 她作为对过去伟大船只的深刻纪念。
lines.raw: She serves as an impressive reminder of the great ships of the past. | 她作为对过去伟大船只的深刻纪念。

lines.english: Before they were replaced by steamships,
lines.chinese: 在它们被蒸汽船取代之前，
lines.raw: Before they were replaced by steamships, | 在它们被蒸汽船取代之前，

lines.english: sailing vessels like the Cutty Sark were used to carry tea from China and wool from Australia.
lines.chinese: 像卡蒂萨克号这样的帆船被用来从中国运茶，从澳大利亚运羊毛。
lines.raw: sailing vessels like the Cutty Sark were used to carry tea from China and wool from Australia. | 像卡蒂萨克号这样的帆船被用来从中国运茶，从澳大利亚运羊毛。

lines.english: The Cutty Sark was one of the fastest sailing ships that has ever been built.
lines.chinese: 卡蒂萨克号是建造过的最快的帆船之一。
lines.raw: The Cutty Sark was one of the fastest sailing ships that has ever been built. | 卡蒂萨克号是建造过的最快的帆船之一。

lines.english: The only other ship to match her was the Thermopylae.
lines.chinese: 唯一能与她匹敌的船是“特莫皮莱”号。
lines.raw: The only other ship to match her was the Thermopylae. | 唯一能与她匹敌的船是“特莫皮莱”号。

lines.english: Both these ships set out from Shanghai on June 18th, 1872 on an exciting race to England.
lines.chinese: 这两艘船于1872年6月18日从上海启程，进行一场激动人心的英国竞赛。
lines.raw: Both these ships set out from Shanghai on June 18th, 1872 on an exciting race to England. | 这两艘船于1872年6月18日从上海启程，进行一场激动人心的英国竞赛。

lines.english: This race, which went on for exactly four months, was the last of its kind.
lines.chinese: 这场持续了整整四个月的比赛是同类比赛中的最后一场。
lines.raw: This race, which went on for exactly four months, was the last of its kind. | 这场持续了整整四个月的比赛是同类比赛中的最后一场。

lines.english: It marked the end of the great tradition of ships with sails and the beginning of a new era.
lines.chinese: 它标志着帆船伟大传统的结束和新时代的开始。
lines.raw: It marked the end of the great tradition of ships with sails and the beginning of a new era. | 它标志着帆船伟大传统的结束和新时代的开始。

lines.english: The first of the two ships to reach Java after the race had begun was the Thermopylae,
lines.chinese: 比赛开始后，两艘船中最先抵达爪哇的是“特莫皮莱”号，
lines.raw: The first of the two ships to reach Java after the race had begun was the Thermopylae, | 比赛开始后，两艘船中最先抵达爪哇的是“特莫皮莱”号，

lines.english: but on the Indian Ocean, the Cutty Sark took the lead.
lines.chinese: 但在印度洋上，“卡蒂萨克”号取得了领先。
lines.raw: but on the Indian Ocean, the Cutty Sark took the lead. | 但在印度洋上，“卡蒂萨克”号取得了领先。

lines.english: It seemed certain that she would be the first ship home,
lines.chinese: 似乎她肯定会是第一艘抵达的船，
lines.raw: It seemed certain that she would be the first ship home, | 似乎她肯定会是第一艘抵达的船，

lines.english: but during the race she had a lot of bad luck.
lines.chinese: 但在比赛中她遭遇了很多厄运。
lines.raw: but during the race she had a lot of bad luck. | 但在比赛中她遭遇了很多厄运。

lines.english: In August, she was struck by a very heavy storm during which her rudder was torn away.
lines.chinese: 八月，她遭遇了一场特大风暴，船舵被刮走了。
lines.raw: In August, she was struck by a very heavy storm during which her rudder was torn away. | 八月，她遭遇了一场特大风暴，船舵被刮走了。

lines.english: The Cutty Sark rolled from side to side and it became impossible to steer her.
lines.chinese: 卡蒂萨克号左右摇晃，无法掌舵。
lines.raw: The Cutty Sark rolled from side to side and it became impossible to steer her. | 卡蒂萨克号左右摇晃，无法掌舵。

lines.english: A temporary rudder was made on board from spare planks and it was fitted with great difficulty.
lines.chinese: 船上用备用木板做了一个临时船舵，并费了很大力气才安装好。
lines.raw: A temporary rudder was made on board from spare planks and it was fitted with great difficulty. | 船上用备用木板做了一个临时船舵，并费了很大力气才安装好。

lines.english: This greatly reduced the speed of the ship, for there was a danger that if she travelled too quickly,
lines.chinese: 这大大降低了船速，因为存在一个危险，如果她航行过快，
lines.raw: This greatly reduced the speed of the ship, for there was a danger that if she travelled too quickly, | 这大大降低了船速，因为存在一个危险，如果她航行过快，

lines.english: this rudder would be torn away as well.
lines.chinese: 这个船舵也会被刮走。
lines.raw: this rudder would be torn away as well. | 这个船舵也会被刮走。

lines.english: Because of this, the Cutty Sark lost her lead.
lines.chinese: 因此，卡蒂萨克号失去了领先地位。
lines.raw: Because of this, the Cutty Sark lost her lead. | 因此，卡蒂萨克号失去了领先地位。

lines.english: After crossing the Equator, the captain called in at a port to have a new rudder fitted,
lines.chinese: 越过赤道后，船长在一个港口停靠，安装了一个新船舵，
lines.raw: After crossing the Equator, the captain called in at a port to have a new rudder fitted, | 越过赤道后，船长在一个港口停靠，安装了一个新船舵，

lines.english: but by now the Thermopylae was over five hundred miles ahead.
lines.chinese: 但此时“特莫皮莱”号已经领先五百多英里了。
lines.raw: but by now the Thermopylae was over five hundred miles ahead. | 但此时“特莫皮莱”号已经领先五百多英里了。

lines.english: Though the new rudder was fitted at tremendous speed, it was impossible for the Cutty Sark to win.
lines.chinese: 尽管新船舵以惊人的速度安装好了，卡蒂萨克号还是不可能赢得比赛。
lines.raw: Though the new rudder was fitted at tremendous speed, it was impossible for the Cutty Sark to win. | 尽管新船舵以惊人的速度安装好了，卡蒂萨克号还是不可能赢得比赛。

lines.english: She arrived in England a week after the Thermopylae.
lines.chinese: 她在“特莫皮莱”号之后一周抵达英国。
lines.raw: She arrived in England a week after the Thermopylae. | 她在“特莫皮莱”号之后一周抵达英国。

lines.english: Even this was remarkable, considering that she had had so many delays.
lines.chinese: 考虑到她有如此多的延误，即使这样也值得称赞。
lines.raw: Even this was remarkable, considering that she had had so many delays. | 考虑到她有如此多的延误，即使这样也值得称赞。

lines.english: There is no doubt that if she had not lost her rudder she would have won the race easily.
lines.chinese: 毫无疑问，如果她没有失去船舵，她会轻易赢得比赛。
lines.raw: There is no doubt that if she had not lost her rudder she would have won the race easily. | 毫无疑问，如果她没有失去船舵，她会轻易赢得比赛。
